title: 9 Herbal Teas For Coughing Fits
intro_desc: Smokers often experience coughing fits due to the irritation caused by tar and other chemicals in tobacco smoke. These irritants inflame the airways, leading to a persistent cough that can be uncomfortable and disrupt daily life. This can be especially challenging for smokers who have to deal with coughing fits throughout the day, affecting their work, relationships, and overall well-being. The constant coughing can also lead to fatigue, throat discomfort, and difficulty sleeping. As a result, smokers may feel isolated and struggle to manage their condition.  To alleviate these symptoms, some smokers turn to herbal teas as a natural remedy. These teas are made from plants that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the irritated airways and reduce coughing fits. By drinking these teas, smokers can experience relief from their symptoms and improve their quality of life. The soothing effects of these teas can help calm the throat and lungs, allowing smokers to breathe more easily and manage their coughing fits more effectively.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea might help soothe a cough. Some teas, like thyme and ginger, have properties that can ease irritation in the throat. When you drink these teas, the warm liquid can calm your throat and reduce the urge to cough. This might help you feel better and stop coughing fits from forming.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe a cough and reduce the frequency of coughing fits. Some herbal teas, like thyme and eucalyptus, have natural decongestant properties that can ease congestion and calm the throat. These teas can be a gentle way to manage coughs and promote relaxation.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas daily for coughing fits can be safe if you choose teas that are gentle and natural. Look for teas made from ingredients like ginger, chamomile, and peppermint, which can help soothe your throat and calm your cough. However, be cautious of teas that contain strong herbs or additives, as they might irritate your throat or interact with other medications.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to help with coughing fits within a few hours to a day after drinking them. Some people notice relief from congestion and coughs faster, while others may take a bit longer to feel the effects. It really depends on the individual and the specific tea being used.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for coughing fits can be soothing at any time of day. However, if you want to get the most out of it, try having a cup before bedtime. Your body can focus on healing while you sleep, and waking up with a clearer throat is a nice bonus.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for coughing fits. Some herbs, like ginseng and garlic, can thin blood and make medications work too strong. Others, like licorice root, can raise blood pressure and worsen conditions like heart problems or high blood pressure. This can cause problems, but it depends on the specific herbs and medications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have a cough, herbal teas can be tempting to try. However, some herbs can be too strong for your baby. For example, peppermint and ginger are usually safe, but sage and pennyroyal can be too strong. It's best to stick with what you know and what's recommended for pregnant women.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing relief for coughing fits, and some are safe to drink while breastfeeding. However, be cautious of teas that contain high amounts of caffeine or strong herbs like peppermint, which can pass into breast milk. Choose teas that are naturally gentle and low in caffeine, like chamomile or lemon balm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like cineole and eucalyptol, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These help to ease congestion and reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and talk. When you're experiencing a coughing fit, drinking Eucalyptus globulus tea can help to calm your respiratory system and provide temporary relief from the discomfort. To get the most out of this tea, try steeping one teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.  You can also add honey or lemon to your tea to make it more palatable. If you're taking medications for other health conditions, consult your doctor before using Eucalyptus globulus tea to avoid any potential interactions.  After drinking the tea, you may notice a reduction in the frequency and severity of your coughing fits. Your airways may feel clearer, and you might be able to breathe more easily. However, if your cough persists or worsens, consult your doctor to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains a number of compounds that can help alleviate coughing fits. The active constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. This means that they can help reduce inflammation in the airways and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, which can help calm a persistent cough. If you're experiencing a coughing fit, try taking a warm cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea. You can steep the tea for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds. To get the most out of the tea, drink it when you feel the cough coming on, rather than waiting for it to pass. You can also try drinking the tea before bed if you're experiencing a persistent cough at night. When you take Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for coughing fits, you can expect to feel a reduction in coughing within a few hours. The tea may also help you feel more comfortable and relaxed, as the anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce any discomfort or pain in the throat. However, if your cough persists or worsens, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it less irritated and less likely to trigger coughing fits. The caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid also have antimicrobial effects, which can help fight off infections that may be causing the cough. When taking Echinacea purpurea tea to help with coughing fits, it's best to drink 1-2 cups per day, 2-3 times a day, and to use a strong tea infusion or a tincture. You can also add honey to soothe the throat and make the tea more palatable. As you drink the tea, you may notice a decrease in the frequency and severity of your coughing fits within a few days, and your body may be better able to fight off any underlying infections. However, if your cough persists or worsens, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve coughing fits. The active constituents in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the body, thus reducing congestion and coughing. If you're experiencing persistent coughing fits, try brewing a warm cup of Zingiber officinale tea by steeping a teaspoon of fresh ginger in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. To get the most out of this remedy, try drinking the tea at least 30 minutes before meals and at bedtime. You can also use it as a preventative measure by drinking it once a day to keep your respiratory system healthy. Taking Zingiber officinale tea may lead to an immediate reduction in coughing frequency and severity, as well as relief from congestion and a stuffy nose. You may also experience a soothing, warming sensation in your throat and chest as the tea works to reduce inflammation and promote mucus clearance.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas contains properties and constituents that help to soothe and calm a coughing fit. The tea is rich in flavonoids, specifically quercetin, and phenolic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relax the cough reflex. The tea also contains saponins, which have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. When taking Sambucus nigra tea for coughing fits, it's recommended to drink 1-2 cups, 2-3 times a day, as hot tea to help loosen mucus and soothe the throat. You can also add honey to sweeten the tea and help to calm the cough. If you're experiencing a severe or persistent cough, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. Taking Sambucus nigra tea as directed may help to reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits, allowing you to breathe more easily and recover from illness more quickly.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to calm the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and lessening the severity of coughing fits. The tea also contains vitamins and minerals like potassium and iron, which can help to soothe a sore throat and promote healing. If you're experiencing persistent or severe coughing fits, try drinking a warm cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea after meals and before bedtime. You can also try adding honey or lemon to the tea to make it more palatable.  As you drink the tea, you may start to feel a calming effect on your airways, and your coughing fits may become less frequent and less severe. Your throat may start to feel soothed and less irritated, and you may begin to sleep better at night. However, if your coughing persists or worsens, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce the severity of coughing fits by fighting off the underlying infection and soothing the irritated airways. Thymol, in particular, has been shown to have expectorant properties, which means it helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up.  When you have a coughing fit, try sipping on a warm cup of Thymus vulgaris tea. To make the tea, steep one teaspoon of dried thyme leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and add honey to taste, if desired. Drink the tea while it's still warm to help calm your cough.  As you take action, you can expect the following outcomes: the severity of your coughing fits may decrease, and you may experience relief from congestion and cough. The anti-inflammatory properties in the tea may also help reduce any discomfort or pain in your chest or throat. However, it's essential to note that if your cough persists or worsens, you should consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce congestion in the chest and throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing fits. When you drink Mentha x piperita tea, the menthol soothes the throat and relaxes the muscles, reducing the urge to cough. To use Mentha x piperita tea for coughing fits, try sipping on a warm cup of the tea 2-3 times a day. You can also add a few drops of menthol oil to a humidifier or inhale it directly to help open up airways. As you take action, you may start to feel a decrease in congestion and a reduction in coughing fits within a few hours. You may also find that your throat and chest feel more relaxed, and you're able to breathe more easily. If you're experiencing severe or persistent coughing fits, it's always a good idea to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for coughing fits

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contains compounds like camphor, borneol, and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits. When you take Rosmarinus officinalis teas for coughing fits, try to drink it warm and on an empty stomach to enhance absorption. Start with a small amount, about 1 teaspoon of dried herbs per cup of boiling water, and gradually increase the dose as needed. You can also add honey to make it more palatable. As the tea works its way through your system, you may notice a decrease in coughing fits and an improvement in your overall respiratory health. However, if your cough is severe or persistent, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for coughing fits
